---
title: "GMBC_data analysis"
author: "AL"
date: "2024-10-22"
output: pdf_document
--- 
       
# DATA READING AND CLEANING
  
    
Set Up 
```{r libraries, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)

#General
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(glue)
library(openxlsx)

#Plotting
library(ggplot2)
library(ggtext)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
library(cowplot)

#Models
library(Publish)
library(purrr)
library(broom)

#Stats
library(tableone)
library(rstatix)
library(Maaslin2)

#WGS
library(vegan)

```
  
```{r estrobolome targets, control ids, WGS filter}

## Estrobolome data
estrobolome_targets <- read_csv("Estrobolome targets_2025-09-04.csv")

## List of Plate_IDs for Control Samples
technical_ids <- c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408", #plate1 012_T1-T3
                   "GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807", #plate2 079_T1-T3
                   "GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
                  "GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415",#plate4 012_T4-T6
                  "GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402", #plate4 079_T4-T6
                  "GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)

control_ids <- c("GMBC_00508","GMBC_00609","GMBC_00723","GMBC_00416", #plate1 NC_01-04
                  "GMBC_00212","GMBC_00801","GMBC_00305","GMBC_00902", #plate2 NC_05-08
                  "GMBC_01002","GMBC_01114","GMBC_01201", #plate3 NC_09-11
                 "GMBC_01414","GMBC_01317") #plate4 NC_12,NC_13


## Filter WGS data by a meta (epi) variable 
wgs_filter <- function(data, variable, var_value) {
  #convert alpha to a symbol for NSE
  alpha <- rlang::ensym(variable)
  #data modifications
  gmbc_data %>% 
    filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>%
    #pull out needed meta from gmbc_data
    select(plate_ID, !!alpha) %>%
    #add onto the metaphlan data
    merge(x=data, y=., by="plate_ID", all.x=TRUE) %>%
    #filter by the added meta variable
    filter(!!alpha==var_value) %>% 
    #drop meta var so only meteagenomic
    select(-!!alpha)
}


## Filter metabolite data by collection part
metabo_filter.p1 <- function(metabo_data) {
  metabo_data %>%
    merge(gmbc_data %>% select(study_id, collection_part), by = "study_id", all.x = TRUE) %>%
    filter(collection_part == "1") %>%
    select(-collection_part)
}


``` 
   
gmbc_data (REDCap, CDHQIII)             
```{r gmbc_data read in, impute}

#Read in gmbc_data from Data Cleaning.Rmd  
gmbc_data <- read.csv("gmbc_data_2025-08-22.csv") %>% 
  #filter for only part 1
  filter(collection_part == 1)

## Converting Data to Factor
gmbc_data <- gmbc_data %>%
  mutate(
    #selecting redcap variables only
      across(
      .cols = study_id:t_biol.occur,
      .fns = ~ if (!(cur_column() %in% 
              #numerical variables (for NOT in this list)
              c("s_age","s_height_cm","s_bmi","d_household_adults", "d_household_children","METmin",
              "rh_menarchy_age","s_menopause_age","rh_contraceptive_totalyr","rh_pregnancy_num",
              "rh_pregnancy_live","rh_pregnancy_age_first","rh_breastfeed_months",
               "rh_pregnancy_age_last","t_diameter.avg") 
        )) {
          as.factor(.)
        } else {
          .
        }
    ),
  #re-leveling factors
  ethnic_collapsed = factor(ethnic_collapsed, 
      levels = c("White","Asian","Other")),
  d_education_collapsed = factor(d_education_collapsed, 
    levels = c("<= High school","Post-high school training","Bachelor's","Postgraduate")),
  d_income_collapsed = factor(d_income_collapsed, 
      levels = c("49k","50k","100k","150k","PNA")) 
  )

## Updating GMBC variables for adjustment
gmbc_data <- gmbc_data %>% 
  mutate(
    #updates for control who became a case
    plate_ID = case_when(
      study_id == "GMBC_048" ~ "GMBC_00803",
      study_id == "GMBC_048_E2" ~ "GMBC_01318",
      TRUE ~ plate_ID ),
    #age at first pregnancy
    rh_pregnancy_age_first_cat = case_when(
        (rh_pregnancy_age_first >= 30) ~ as.factor(3), #geriatric pregnancy
        (rh_pregnancy_age_first < 30 & rh_pregnancy_age_first >= 25) ~ as.factor(2),
        (rh_pregnancy_age_first < 25) ~ as.factor(1),
        TRUE ~ as.factor(0) ),
    #smoking never / ever
    sm_summary = case_when(
        (sm_summary == 0) ~ as.factor(0),
        (sm_summary %in% c(1,2)) ~ as.factor(1),
        TRUE ~ NA ),
    #education level
    d_education_collapsed = case_when(
      (d_education_collapsed %in% c("<= High school","Post-high school training") ) ~ as.factor("< Bachelor's"),
      TRUE ~ d_education_collapsed ),
    d_education_collapsed = factor(d_education_collapsed), #refactor to collapse levels
    #ethnicity
    ethnic_collapsed = case_when(
      ethnic_collapsed %in% c("Asian","Other") ~ as.factor("Non-White"),
      TRUE ~ ethnic_collapsed),
    ethnic_collapsed = factor(ethnic_collapsed), #refactor to collapse levels
    
  #Dietary Vars (DENSITY ADJUSTMENT)
  Total.dietary.fibre.g_density = (Total.dietary.fibre.g / Energy.kcal) * 1000,
  Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g_density = 
    (Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g / Energy.kcal) * 1000,
  Total.sugar.g_density = (Total.sugar.g / Energy.kcal) * 1000
)


``` 
 
MetaPhlAn4 (stool)         
```{r metaphlan data read and cleaning}

## Read in the data
metaphlan4_all <- 
  #force three cols to properly read in
  read.table("profiled_metagenome.tsv", sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, col.names = c("plate_ID", "clade_name", "clade_taxid", "relative_abundance", "coverage", "estimated_number_of_reads_from_the_clade"), quote = "", comment.char = "") %>%
  #drop front matter
  slice(-(1:329)) %>% 
  mutate(name = str_extract(clade_name, "[^|]+$"),
         #need to convert to numeric
         across(relative_abundance:estimated_number_of_reads_from_the_clade, as.numeric)) %>% 
  #derive rank
  mutate(rank = case_when(
    grepl("k__", name) ~ "kingdom",
    grepl("p__", name) ~ "phylum",
    grepl("c__", name) ~ "class",
    grepl("o__", name) ~ "order",
    grepl("f__", name) ~ "family",
    grepl("g__", name) ~ "genus",
    grepl("s__", name) ~ "species",
    grepl("t__", name) ~ "subspecies",
    TRUE ~ "other"
  ))


## Data Selection

#Data selection
metaphlan4 <- metaphlan4_all %>% 
  # Drop the negative and technical controls
  filter(!plate_ID %in% control_ids) %>%
  filter(!plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  # SELECT PART 1 SAMPLES ONLY
  wgs_filter(., collection_part, 1)

#The third percentile of data = 0.00078 (which is almost 0.001% as used in Robertson 2023), 4=0.00104, 5=0.00132
min_abundance_threshold <- quantile(metaphlan4 %>% pull(relative_abundance), probs = 0.03)
min_abundance_threshold
#Calculating frequency of taxa present
filtered_taxa <- metaphlan4 %>%
  #binary presence/absence matrix
  mutate(presence = ifelse(is.na(relative_abundance), FALSE, relative_abundance > min_abundance_threshold)) %>% #0
  #calc presence frequency for each taxon
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(
    presence_count = sum(presence), #243 is the max (should be 241?)
    frequency = presence_count / n_distinct(metaphlan4$plate_ID) #presence/total count to get %
  )
#Filter taxa based on prevalence thresholds
filtered_taxa10 <- filtered_taxa %>% filter(frequency >= 0.1) %>% pull(name)
#Data Filtering
metaphlan4_long <- metaphlan4 %>% 
  filter(name %in% filtered_taxa10) %>% 
  arrange(plate_ID)


## Rename the subspecies to include the species in the name (more informative)
metaphlan4_long <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  mutate(name = if_else( rank == "subspecies", #if subspecies level
                         str_extract(clade_name, "s__[^|]+\\|t__[^|]+"), #rename to include species
                         name) ) #otherwise leave


## Transforming to wider form
metaphlan4_wide <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  filter(rank %in% c("species","genus","subspecies")) %>%
  pivot_wider(., 
    id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
    names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
    values_from=relative_abundance,
    values_fill = 0)  #covert NA relative abundance to 0s

## Transforming to wider form for SPECIES data
metaphlan4_wide.s <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  filter(rank=="species") %>%
  pivot_wider(., 
    id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
    names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
    values_from=relative_abundance,
    values_fill = 0)  #covert NA relative abundance to 0s

## Transforming to wider form for GENUS data
metaphlan4_wide.g <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  filter(rank=="genus") %>%
  pivot_wider(., 
    id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
    names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
    values_from=relative_abundance,
    values_fill = 0)  #covert NA relative abundance to 0s

## Transforming to wider form for SUBSPECIES data
metaphlan4_wide.t <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  filter(rank=="subspecies") %>%
  pivot_wider(., 
    id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
    names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
    values_from=relative_abundance,
    values_fill = 0)  #covert NA relative abundance to 0s


```   

HUMAnN3 (stool)
```{r humann.path read and clean}

## Read in cleaned data set
humann.path_nopipe <- read.csv("humann.path_nopipe.csv") %>% 
  # Drop the negative and technical controls
  filter(!plate_ID %in% control_ids) %>%
  filter(!plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  # select baseline samples only
  wgs_filter(., collection_part, 1)  %>% 
  rename(relative_abundance = value)


## Data Filtering

#third percentile of data
min_abundance_threshold <- humann.path_nopipe %>% 
  filter(!name %in% c("UNMAPPED","UNINTEGRATED")) %>% 
  pull(relative_abundance) %>% 
  quantile(., probs = 0.03)

#calculating frequency of taxa present
filtered_path5 <- humann.path_nopipe %>%
  #binary presence/absence matrix
  mutate(presence = ifelse(is.na(relative_abundance), FALSE, relative_abundance > min_abundance_threshold)) %>%
  #calc presence frequency for each taxon
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(
    presence_count = sum(presence),
    #presence/total count to get %
    frequency = presence_count / n_distinct(humann.path_nopipe$plate_ID)
  ) %>%
  #filter taxa based on the 5% threshold
  filter(frequency >= 0.05) %>%
  #pull out names
  pull(name)


#apply
humann.path_long <- humann.path_nopipe %>% 
  #remove unintegrated and unmapped samples
  filter(!name %in% c("UNMAPPED","UNINTEGRATED")) %>% 
  #filtering dataframe for >5% frequency
  filter(name %in% filtered_path5)

## Transforming to wider form
humann.path_wide <- humann.path_long %>% 
  pivot_wider(id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
              names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
              values_from=relative_abundance,
              values_fill = 0)  #covert NA relative abundance to 0s


``` 

```{r humann.enzymes_nopipe read and clean}

## Read in cleaned data set
humann.enzymes_nopipe <- read.csv("humann.enzymes_nopipe.csv") %>% 
  # Drop the negative and technical controls
  filter(!plate_ID %in% control_ids) %>%
  filter(!plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  # select baseline samples only
  wgs_filter(., collection_part, 1)  %>% 
  rename(relative_abundance = value)
  

## Data Filtering

#identify third percentile of data
min_abundance_threshold <- quantile(humann.enzymes_nopipe %>% pull(relative_abundance), probs = 0.03)

#calculate frequency of taxa present
filtered_enzyme5 <- humann.enzymes_nopipe %>%
  #binary presence/absence matrix
  mutate(presence = ifelse(is.na(relative_abundance), FALSE, relative_abundance > min_abundance_threshold)) %>%
  #calc presence frequency for each taxon
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarize(
    presence_count = sum(presence),
    #presence/total count to get %
    frequency = presence_count / n_distinct(humann.enzymes_nopipe$plate_ID)
  ) %>% 
  #filter taxa based on the 5% threshold
  filter(frequency >= 0.05) %>%
  #pull out names
  pull(name)

#applying filter for >5% frequency
humann.enzymes_long <- humann.enzymes_nopipe %>%
  filter(name %in% filtered_enzyme5)

## Transforming to wider form
humann.enzymes_wide <- humann.enzymes_long %>% 
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
              names_from = name, #Enzyme name has special characters that crash
              values_from = relative_abundance,
              values_fill = 0)  #covert NA relative abundance to 0s


```

Metabolomics (blood & stool)   
```{r metabo plasma - reading and cleaning}

#Read in metabolomics abundances
plasma_abund <- read.csv("GMBC_Metabolite_Results.plasma_2025-04-30.csv") %>% 
  # SELECT PART 1 SAMPLES ONLY
  metabo_filter.p1()

## Calculating prevalence frequency of metabolites present
filtered_metabo10 <- plasma_abund %>%
  pivot_longer(-study_id, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "relab") %>%
  mutate(presence = ifelse(is.na(relab), FALSE, relab > 0)) %>%
  group_by(Metabolite) %>%
  summarize(frequency = sum(presence) / n_distinct(plasma_abund$study_id)) %>%
  filter(frequency >= 0.10) %>%
  pull(Metabolite)


## Data Filtering >10% prevalence frequency
metabo.plasma_wide <- plasma_abund %>%
  select(study_id, all_of(filtered_metabo10)) %>% 
  merge(., gmbc_data %>% select(study_id), by="study_id", all.x=T)

## Long form data
metabo.plasma_long <- metabo.plasma_wide %>% 
  pivot_longer(-study_id, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "relative_abundance")


``` 
  
```{r metabo stool - reading and cleaning}

#Clean up the study codes
stool_abund <- read.csv("GMBC_Metabolite_Results.stool_2025-04-30.csv") %>% 
  # SELECT PART 1 SAMPLES ONLY
  metabo_filter.p1()


## Calculating prevalence frequency of metabolites present
filtered_metabo10 <- stool_abund %>%
  pivot_longer(-study_id, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "relab") %>%
  mutate(presence = ifelse(is.na(relab), FALSE, relab > 0)) %>%
  group_by(Metabolite) %>%
  summarize(frequency = sum(presence) / n_distinct(stool_abund$study_id)) %>%
  filter(frequency >= 0.10) %>%
  pull(Metabolite)


## Data Filtering >10% prevalence frequency (metabo_wide is filtered)
metabo.stool_wide <- stool_abund %>%
  select(study_id, all_of(filtered_metabo10)) %>% 
  merge(., gmbc_data %>% select(study_id), by="study_id", all.x=T)

## Long form data
metabo.stool_long <- metabo.stool_wide %>% 
  pivot_longer(-study_id, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "relative_abundance")


``` 
   

   
# ANALYSES FUNCTIONS 

```{r alpha and beta diversity - functions (4) }

#alpha is within sample diversity - what and how much is there in a sample
#beta is between sample diversity - how similar is one sample to another
 
## Calculate Alpha Diversity with Sig Tests (Wilcoxon Rank Sum)
run_alpha_diversity <- function(data_long, metaphlan=FALSE) {
  
  ## Calculate alpha diversity as new cols
  alpha_data <- data_long %>% 
    #conditional filtering, if metaphlan==T select species, default is FALSE
    { if (metaphlan) filter(., rank == "subspecies") else . } %>%
    #{ if (metaphlan) filter(., rank == "genus") else . } %>%
    group_by(plate_ID) %>% #all species in a sample
    #calculate alpha metrics
    summarize(shannon = diversity(relative_abundance, "shannon"),
              richness = specnumber(relative_abundance)) %>% 
    #merge alpha metrics onto gmbc_data
    merge(gmbc_data %>% select(plate_ID, case_con),
          ., by="plate_ID", all.x = TRUE)
  
  ## Sig testing for case VS controls (wilcoxon rank sum) 
  
    #SHANNON
    #calculate group medians alpha div
    meds <- alpha_data %>% 
      #filter(shannon > 0) %>% #only include where alpha data
      group_by(case_con) %>% #group by cancer status
      summarise(median_alpha = median(shannon), .groups = "drop") #calculate group medians
    #calculating p values using wilcoxon rank sum test
    p.shannon <- wilcox.test(shannon ~ case_con, data = alpha_data) #%>%filter(shannon>0))
    #print out metrics
    print(glue("The group medians of SHANNON diversity:
             Controls = {meds$median_alpha[meds$case_con == 0]}
             Cases = {meds$median_alpha[meds$case_con == 1]}
             Abs median difference: {abs(diff(meds$median_alpha))}
             The p value (Wilcoxon): {p.shannon$p.value}"))

    #RICHNESS
    #calculate group medians alpha div
    meds <- alpha_data %>% 
      #filter(richness > 0) %>% #only include where alpha data
      group_by(case_con) %>% #group by cancer status
      summarise(median_alpha = median(richness), .groups = "drop") #calculate group medians
    #calculating p values using wilcoxon rank sum test
    p.richness <- wilcox.test(richness ~ case_con, data = alpha_data)#%>%filter(richness>0))
    #print out metrics
    print(glue("The group medians of RICHNESS:
             Controls = {meds$median_alpha[meds$case_con == 0]}
             Cases = {meds$median_alpha[meds$case_con == 1]}
             Abs median difference: {abs(diff(meds$median_alpha))}
             The p value (Wilcoxon): {p.richness$p.value}"))
  
  return(list(alpha_data = alpha_data, 
            p_shannon = p.shannon$p.value, 
            p_richness = p.richness$p.value))
}

## Plot Individual Alpha Plots for Shannon + Richness
plot_alpha_diversity <- function(data, alpha, y_title, p.val, x.size, y.size) {
  
  alpha <- rlang::ensym(alpha) # Convert alpha to a symbol for NSE
  
  alpha.plot <- data %>% 
  filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=as.factor(case_con), y=!!alpha, color=as.factor(case_con))) + 
    geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha=0.3, show.legend = FALSE) + 
    labs(x=element_blank(), y=y_title) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("0"="darkgray", "1"="blue")) +
    theme(panel.background = element_blank(),
          plot.background = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
          axis.ticks.x = element_blank()) + 
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA), 
          panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines")) +
    annotate("text", x=x.size, y=y.size, label = paste0("p = ", round(p.val, digits=4)),
             color = "black", size = 3)
  
  return(alpha.plot)
}

## Run Beta Diversity (Bray-Curtis Distances), Sig test PERMANOVA, PCA, plot
plot_beta_diversity <- function(data_wide, x.size, y.size) {

  ## Calculate Bray-Curtis Distance Matrix
  dist_mat1 <- data_wide %>% 
    arrange(plate_ID) %>% 
    column_to_rownames("plate_ID") %>% #turning column values to name
    as.matrix() %>% #convert to matrix
    vegdist(method = "bray") #calc matrix of distances using vegan function


  ## Significant testing between groups (PERMANOVA)
    #pulling out grouping variable
    groups1 <- merge(
        data_wide %>% select(plate_ID),
        gmbc_data %>% select(plate_ID, case_con, collection_part),
        by="plate_ID"
      ) %>% 
      arrange(plate_ID) %>% 
      pull(case_con) %>% #pull out the case_con variables
      factor(levels = c("0", "1")) #set the factor levels
    #apply permanova function
    beta_p <- adonis2(dist_mat1 ~ groups1)$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    beta_R2 <- adonis2(dist_mat1 ~ groups1)$`R2`[1]


  ## Calculating PCOAs
    #apply pcoa
    beta_pcoa <- cmdscale(dist_mat1, #pcoa is list with $points (PCs per sample)
                       k = 8, #N-1 PCs (default 2)
                       eig = TRUE, # $eig (explained variation), $x (null), $ac, $GOF
                       add = TRUE) #added add=T from Sandeep
    #convert eigenvalues to percentage
    beta_perc_exp <- 100 * beta_pcoa$eig / sum(beta_pcoa$eig)
    #turn pcoa points from list to dataframe
    beta_pcoa.df <- as.data.frame(beta_pcoa$points) %>% #turn list component to dataframe
      rownames_to_column(var = "plate_ID") %>% #study id stored as row_name
      rename_with(~paste0("PC", gsub("V", "", .)), starts_with("V")) %>% #col names from V to PC
      merge(gmbc_data, ., by="plate_ID", all.x = TRUE) #merge PC values onto data

    #calculate the median coordinates for each group
    medians <- beta_pcoa.df %>%
      filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>%
      group_by(case_con) %>%
      summarize(med_PC1 = median(PC1), med_PC2 = median(PC2))

  ## Plotting PCOAs
  beta_plot <- beta_pcoa.df %>% 
    filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>% 
    ggplot(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = case_con)) + 
    geom_point() +
    stat_ellipse(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2), level = 0.95) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("0" = "darkgray", "1" = "blue")) + 
    labs(x = glue("PC 1 ({round(beta_perc_exp[1], digits = 1)}% exp var)"),
         y = paste0(
                "<span style='font-size:10pt'>Beta Diversity</span><br>",
                "<span style='font-size:8pt'>PC 2 (", round(beta_perc_exp[2], 1), "% exp var)</span>"
              ),
           #glue("PC 2 ({round(beta_perc_exp[2], digits = 1)}% exp var)"),
         color = "Case Status") + 
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          axis.title.y = element_markdown(),
          panel.background = element_blank(),
          plot.background = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
          panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines")) +
    annotate("text", x = x.size, y = y.size,
             label = glue("p = {round(beta_p, 3)}\nR2 = {round(beta_R2, digits = 3)}"),
             color = "black", size = 3) +
    #add lines from each point to the median of its group with matching colors
    geom_segment(data = beta_pcoa.df %>% 
                   filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>% 
                   left_join(medians, by = "case_con"),
                 aes(x = med_PC1, y = med_PC2, 
                     xend = PC1, yend = PC2, 
                     color = case_con),
                 linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.5) +
    #add median points with matching colors
    geom_point(data = medians, 
               aes(x = med_PC1, y = med_PC2, color = case_con), 
               size = 3, shape = 4)  # Shape 4 is an 'x' marker

  return(beta_plot)
  }

## Plot Alpha and Beta Diversity - Unified Plot
plot_alpha_beta <- function(alpha_data, beta_plot, alpha_plot1, alpha_plot2, letter1, letter2, letter3, filename) {
  
  # Open a PNG device
  png(filename, width = 4 * 600, height = 4 * 600, res = 600, bg = "white")
  
  ##Pull out a Legend
    #build shannon plot
    ap1 <- alpha_data %>% 
      filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>% 
      mutate(case_con = factor(case_con, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c('Control', 'Case'))) %>% 
      ggplot(aes(x=case_con, y=shannon, color=case_con)) + 
        scale_color_manual(values = c("Control"="darkgray", "Case"="blue")) +
        geom_boxplot() +
        labs(color = "Cancer Status") + theme(panel.background = element_blank())
    #pulling out legend
    tmp <- ggplot_gtable(ggplot_build(ap1))
    leg <- which(sapply(tmp$grobs, function(x) x$name) == "guide-box")
    legend <- tmp$grobs[[leg]]
  
  ##Build the Bottom Row at a different width
  bottom_row <- arrangeGrob(legend, beta_plot, widths = c(1, 2))
  
  ##Arrange Plots as a Grid 
  grid.arrange(
        alpha_plot1,
        alpha_plot2,
        bottom_row,
        layout_matrix = rbind(c(1, 2),
                              c(3, 3)),
        heights = c(1, 1.75))

  ##Adding in A, B, C Text
  grid.text(letter1, x = unit(0.46, "npc"), y = unit(0.71, "npc"), 
            gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))
  grid.text(letter2, x = unit(0.96, "npc"), y = unit(0.71, "npc"), 
            gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))
  grid.text(letter3, x = unit(0.96, "npc"), y = unit(0.14, "npc"), 
            gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))

  dev.off()
  
}


```
   
```{r data transform - function }

transform_metagenomics <- function(data_wide, merge = TRUE) {
  
  #Calculate the pseudocount as half the smallest non-zero value across all features
  pseudocount <- 0.5 * min(data_wide %>% 
                             select(-plate_ID) %>% 
                             unlist() %>% 
                             .[. > 0], 
                           na.rm = TRUE)
  
  #Transform metagenomic data
  metaphlan.models <- data_wide %>% 
    #CLR transformation
     mutate(across(-plate_ID, 
                   ~ log(. + pseudocount) - mean(log(. + pseudocount), 
                                                    na.rm = TRUE)))# %>% 
    
  # Merge with metadata OR skip
  if (merge) {
    metaphlan.models <- merge(metaphlan.models, gmbc_data, by="plate_ID", all.x = TRUE)
  } else {
    metaphlan.models <- metaphlan.models
  }
  
  return(metaphlan.models)
  
}

transform_metabo <- function(data_wide, remove_outliers = TRUE, merge = TRUE) {
  
  # Step 1: Calculate the pseudocount as half the smallest non-zero value
  pseudocount <- 0.5 * min(
    data_wide %>%
      select(-study_id) %>%
      unlist() %>%
      .[. > 0],
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  
  # Step 2: Log2
  data_log <- data_wide %>%
    mutate(across(-study_id, ~ log2(.x + pseudocount)))

  # Step 3: Apply OR Skip - Mask outliers in raw data (|z| > 4)
  if (remove_outliers) {
    data_clean <- data_log  # copy of raw data
    for (col in names(data_clean)[-1]) {
      #zscore scale
      data_logz <- data_clean %>%
        mutate(across(-study_id, ~ scale(.x)[, 1])) 
      #pull out scores
      z_scores <- data_logz[[col]]
      data_clean[[col]][abs(z_scores) > 4] <- NA
    }
  } else {
    data_clean <- data_log
  }

  # Step 5: Merge with metadata OR skip
  if (merge) {
    data_final <- merge(data_clean, gmbc_data, by = "study_id", all.x = TRUE)
  } else {
    data_final <- data_clean
  }
  
  return(data_final)
  
}

transform_metabo2 <- function(data_wide, remove_outliers = TRUE, transform = TRUE, merge = TRUE) {
  
  # Step 1: Calculate the pseudocount as half the smallest non-zero value
  pseudocount <- 0.5 * min(
    data_wide %>%
      select(-study_id) %>%
      unlist() %>%
      .[. > 0],
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  
  # Step 2: Log2 + z-score for outlier detection
  data_logz <- data_wide %>%
    mutate(across(-study_id, ~ log2(.x + pseudocount))) %>% 
    mutate(across(-study_id, ~ scale(.x)[, 1]))

  # Step 3: Apply OR Skip - Mask outliers in raw data (|z| > 4)
  if (remove_outliers) {
    data_clean <- data_wide  # copy of raw data
    for (col in names(data_clean)[-1]) {
      z_scores <- data_logz[[col]]
      data_clean[[col]][abs(z_scores) > 4] <- NA
    }
  } else {
    data_clean <- data_wide
  }
  
  # Step 4: Conditionally transform based on masked raw stats OR skip
  if (transform) {
    data_transformed <- data_clean %>%
      mutate(across(-study_id, ~ {
        x <- .x
        x_log <- log2(x + pseudocount)
        if (sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / mean(x, na.rm = TRUE) < 0.55) {
          scale(x_log)[, 1]
        } else {
          x_log #otherwise leave as log2
        }
      }))
  } else {
    data_transformed <- data_clean
  }
  
  # Step 5: Merge with metadata OR skip
  if (merge) {
    data_final <- merge(data_transformed, gmbc_data, by = "study_id", all.x = TRUE)
  } else {
    data_final <- data_transformed
  }
  
  return(data_final)
  
}

```
  
```{r adjustment sets}

get_covariates <- function(){
  
  confounder_vars <- c("s_age","s_bmi","ethnic_collapsed","d_education_collapsed", "hormone_current.p1","sm_summary","pa_cat","Alcohol.g"
  )
  
  diet_vars <- c(confounder_vars,
    #energy-adjusted values
    "Total.dietary.fibre.g_density",
    "Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g_density",
    "Total.sugar.g_density"
  )
  
  adjustment_sets <- list(unadj = c(),
                          confounder_vars = confounder_vars,
                          diet_vars = diet_vars)

  return(adjustment_sets)

}

```

Logistic Regression       
```{r iterative modeling - function }
  
## Build the function to apply across multiple variables 
iterative_modeling <- function(column_name, data_set, model_vars) {
  
  #drop any NA for the variable of interest
  data_set <- data_set %>%
    drop_na(!!sym(column_name)) #turn to symbol to ref
  
  # Calculate sample size N
  N <- nrow(data_set)
  
  #construct the formula with the changing column as the first predictor and covariates
  formula <- as.formula(
    paste0(
      #include backticks to allow special characters in the model formula
      "case_con ~ `", column_name, "`",
      #if covariates, add in, otherwise if unadjusted model add nothing
      if (length(model_vars) > 0) paste0("+", paste(model_vars, collapse = " + ")) else ""
      )
    )
  
  #fit the model
  model <- glm(formula, 
               data = data_set, 
               family = binomial)
  
  #print model summary for first taxa, enzyme, metabolite
  if (column_name %in% c("s__Anaerotruncus_rubiinfantis|t__SGB25416","s__Escherichia_coli","g__Alistipes","`Genistein`","`1.1.1.62`","Genistein")) { 
    print(formula)
    print(summary(model))
  }
  
  #pull out results
  result <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE)  %>%
    
    #filter to pull out row of interest
    filter(term == paste0("`", column_name, "`") | term == column_name) %>% 
    
    #calc HR and CIs
    mutate( OR = exp(estimate), #hazard Ratio
            conf_low = exp(conf.low), #confidence Interval Lower Bound
            conf_high = exp(conf.high), #confidence Interval Upper Bound
            OR_CI = paste0(round(OR, 2), " (",
                                round(conf_low, 2), "–",
                                round(conf_high, 2), ")")
            ) %>%
    
    #select relevant terms
    select(term, OR, conf_low, conf_high, OR_CI, p.value)
  
  #add col for sample size
  result$N <- N
  
  #return result
  return(result) 
  
}

```     

```{r iterative modeling - apply } 

library(writexl)
## PRE DEFINE VARIABLES

#predictor_vars
#adjustment_sets 
#dataset.model



## APPLY THE FUNCTION 

apply_iterative.modeling <- function(predictor_vars, adjustment_sets, dataset.model, file_name) {
  
  #Create empty results list
  df_results <- list()
  #Create list to store estrobolome-only results across all models
  estro_summary_list <- list()
    
  # Clean estrobolome list (remove NA)
  estrobolome_list <- c(estrobolome_targets$metaphlan_GMBC,
                        estrobolome_targets$Enzyme_code,
                        estrobolome_targets$Pathway_code)
  estrobolome_list <- Filter(Negate(is.na), estrobolome_list)
  
  #Loop through models for each adjustment level
  for (i in names(adjustment_sets) ) {
    
    #select what to include in the adjustment set
    model_vars = adjustment_sets[[i]]
    
    #Remove NA vars
    dataset.model <- dataset.model %>% 
      filter(if_all(all_of(model_vars), ~ !is.na(.)))
    
    #Apply the function to each column in the selected range and combine results
    log_model.results <- map_dfr(predictor_vars, function(column_name) {
      iterative_modeling(column_name = column_name, 
                         data_set = dataset.model, 
                         model_vars = model_vars)
    }) 
    
    #Calculate a q value (FDR adjustment for multiple testing)
    log_model.results <- log_model.results %>% 
      mutate(q.value = round(p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH"), 3)) %>% 
      arrange(p.value) %>% 
      select(term, OR_CI, p.value, q.value, N, OR, conf_low, conf_high)
    
    # === Filter for estrobolome targets and tag with adjustment ===
    estro_subset <- log_model.results %>%
      filter(str_detect(term, paste(estrobolome_list, collapse = "|"))) %>%
      mutate(adjustment = i)
    
    #Clean up the terms
    log_model.results <- log_model.results %>%
      mutate(term = gsub("\\.", " ", term)) %>%
      mutate(term = gsub("`", "", term))
    estro_subset <- estro_subset %>%
      mutate(term = gsub("\\.", " ", term)) %>%
      mutate(term = gsub("`", "", term))
    
    # Store estrobolome results for this adjustment level
    estro_summary_list[[i]] <- estro_subset
    
    #Store the result in the list with a dynamic sheet name
    df_results[[paste0("Sheet_", i)]] <- log_model.results
    
  } 
  
  # Combine estrobolome summary across adjustment levels
  estrobolome_summary <- bind_rows(estro_summary_list)
      
  # Add estrobolome summary as a sheet in the output
  df_results[["Estrobolome_summary"]] <- estrobolome_summary
  
  #Save to Excel (multiple sheets)
  write_xlsx(df_results, sprintf("Logreg_%s_%s.xlsx", file_name, as.character(Sys.Date())))

}


```
 
 


# SAMPLE CHARACTERISTICS

```{r tableone}

#Variables of interest
table1_vars <- c(
  #demographics
  "s_age","s_height_cm","s_bmi",
  "ethnic_collapsed","d_education_collapsed",
  "health_condition","h_hbp","h_diabetes",
  "family_history",
  "rh_menarchy_age","s_menopause_age",
  "rh_contraceptive", "rh_contraceptive_totalyr",
  "rh_pregnancy_num","rh_pregnancy_live","rh_pregnancy_age_first",
  "rh_pregnancy_age_first_cat","rh_pregnancy_age_last","rh_breastfeed_months",
  "rh_hysterectomy", "rh_ovary",
  "hormone_ever","hormone_current.p1",
  "pa_cat","sm_summary",
  #Diet
  "Alcohol.g","Total.dietary.fibre.g",
  "Total.dietary.fibre.g_density",
  "Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g",
  "Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g_density",
  "Total.sugar.g",
  "Total.sugar.g_density", 
  #Tumor
  "t_er","t_pr","t_her2","t_stage","t_grade","t_foci","t_diameter.avg","t_location",
  "t_rads.occur","t_chemo.occur","t_endo.occur","t_biol.occur","t_bisp.occur",
  "t_rads.days_completed", "t_chemo.days_completed", "t_endo.days_medicated"
)


#hist(gmbc_data$Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g)

#Create list of nonnormal vals to run wilcoxon test on (only works for numerical vals, not categorical)
nonnormal_vars <- c(
  "s_age","s_height_cm","s_bmi","rh_menarchy_age",
  "s_menopause_age","rh_pregnancy_live","rh_breastfeed_months",
  "Alcohol.g","Total.dietary.fibre.g_density",
  "Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g_density","Total.sugar.g_density"
)

#running tableone function
gmbc.table1 <- gmbc_data %>% 
  filter(collection_part==1) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Energy.kcal)) %>% #missing diet removed
  CreateTableOne(data=., 
                 vars= table1_vars,
                 strata="case_con", 
                 test=T, 
                 addOverall=T)
print(gmbc.table1, showAllLevels=T, nonnormal = nonnormal_vars)



```


# METAGENOMICS

## Quality and Controls

```{r metaphlan quality reads}

##### SAMPLE AND CONTROL QUALITY #####

metaphlan4_quality <- 
  #force three cols to properly read in
  read.table("profiled_metagenome_stats.tsv", sep = "\t", fill = TRUE, col.names = c("plate_ID", "reads_processed", "estimated_reads_mapped_to_known_clades"), quote = "", comment.char = "") %>% 
  #drop front matter
  slice(-(1:657)) %>% 
  mutate(
    reads_processed = as.numeric(reads_processed),
    estimated_reads_mapped_to_known_clades = as.numeric(estimated_reads_mapped_to_known_clades),
    non_annotated_reads = reads_processed - estimated_reads_mapped_to_known_clades,
    non_annotated_reads.perc = non_annotated_reads/reads_processed*100
    ) 


# Sample quality
metaphlan4_quality %>% 
  #drop the negative and technical controls
  filter(!plate_ID %in% control_ids) %>%
  filter(!plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  #fiilter part 1 only
  merge(., gmbc_data %>% select(plate_ID, collection_part), by="plate_ID") %>% 
  filter(collection_part == 1) %>% 
  #summarize calcs
  summarize(
    reads_processed.mean = mean(reads_processed),
    reads_processed.sd = sd(reads_processed),
    reads_pr.min = min(reads_processed),
    reads_pr.max = max(reads_processed),
    non_annotated_reads.perc.mean = mean(non_annotated_reads.perc),
    non_annotated_reads.perc.sd = sd(non_annotated_reads.perc),
    non_annot.perc.min = min(non_annotated_reads.perc),
    non_annot.perc.max = max(non_annotated_reads.perc),
  )



# Negative control quality
metaphlan4_quality %>% 
  #filter in negative controls
  filter(plate_ID %in% control_ids) %>%
  #summarize calc
  summarize( no_controls = n(), #number of rows
             reads_processed.max = max(reads_processed),
             reads_processed.min = min(reads_processed),
             reads_processed.mean = mean(reads_processed),
             reads_processed.sd = sd(reads_processed))
  

# Technical Control Quality
metaphlan4_quality %>% 
  #filter in technical controls
  filter(plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  #greating grouping var
  mutate(groups = case_when(
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408") ~ "012_P1", #plate1 012_T1-T3
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807") ~ "079_P1", #plate2 079_T1-T3
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120") ~ "023_P2", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415") ~ "012_P2", #plate4 012_T4-T6
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402") ~ "079_P2", #plate4 079_T4-T6
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") ~ "023_P1", #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)
    TRUE ~ NA )) %>%  
  group_by(groups) %>% 
  #summarize calc
  summarize( no_controls = n(), #number of rows
             reads_processed.max = max(reads_processed),
             reads_processed.min = min(reads_processed),
             reads_processed.mean = mean(reads_processed),
             reads_processed.sd = sd(reads_processed)
             )

##### TECHNICAL CONTROLS TAXONOMY #####

## Total Species Count ####

#select technical data
metaphlan4_all %>% 
  #select tehcnical Controls
  filter(plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  #pivot to wider form
  filter(rank %in% c("species")) %>% #,"genus","subspecies")) %>%
  pivot_wider(., 
    id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
    names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
    values_from=relative_abundance,
    values_fill = 0) %>%   #covert NA relative abundance to 0s
  #calculated row sums
  mutate(total_species_count = rowSums(select(., -1) > 0, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  #greating grouping var
  mutate(groups = case_when(
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408") ~ "012_P1", #plate1 012_T1-T3
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807") ~ "079_P1", #plate2 079_T1-T3
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120") ~ "023_P2", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415") ~ "012_P2", #plate4 012_T4-T6
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402") ~ "079_P2", #plate4 079_T4-T6
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") ~ "023_P1", #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)
    TRUE ~ NA )) %>%  
  group_by(groups) %>%
  #calculate mean and SD of species count per group
  summarise(
    max_species_count = max(total_species_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_species_count = min(total_species_count, na.rm = TRUE), 
    mean_species_count = mean(total_species_count, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_species_count = sd(total_species_count, na.rm = TRUE),
            n = n())



```

```{r Relative abundance plot}

## Phylum Relative abundances ####

phylum_long <- metaphlan4_all %>% 
  #select tehcnical Controls
  filter(plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  #select only phylum level
  filter(rank=="genus") %>% 
  #greating grouping var
  mutate(groups = case_when(
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408") ~ "012_P1", #plate1 012_T1-T3
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807") ~ "079_P1", #plate2 079_T1-T3
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120") ~ "023_P2", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415") ~ "012_P2", #plate4 012_T4-T6
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402") ~ "079_P2", #plate4 079_T4-T6
    plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") ~ "023_P1", #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)
    TRUE ~ NA )) %>%  
  #collection parts
  mutate(collection_part = ifelse(str_detect(groups, "P1"), 1, 2)) %>% 
  # Aggregate and calculate proportions
  group_by(groups, collection_part, plate_ID, name) %>%
  summarize(total_abundance = sum(relative_abundance), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(groups, collection_part, plate_ID) %>%
  mutate(proportion = total_abundance / sum(total_abundance) * 100) %>%
  #drop where less than 5%
  filter(total_abundance >= 3) %>%
  rename(phylum = name) %>%   # Rename 'name' to 'phylum'
  mutate(groups2 = case_when (
            str_detect(groups, "012") ~ "012",
            str_detect(groups, "079") ~ "079",
            str_detect(groups, "023") ~ "023")) %>% 
  mutate(
    groups2 = factor(groups2, levels = c("012","023","079"),
                     labels = c("Participant 12","Participant 23","Participant 79")),
    collection_part = factor(collection_part, levels = c("1","2"),
                             labels = c("Baseline", "Follow Up"))
  ) %>% 
  mutate(
    phylum = ifelse(phylum == "g__Lachnospiraceae_unclassified", "Lachnospiraceae uncl.", phylum),
    phylum = gsub("^(g__|s__)", "", phylum),
    phylum = gsub("_", " ", phylum) 
    )


library(cowplot)
library(ggplot2)

p1 <- ggplot(
   phylum_long %>% filter(collection_part == "Baseline"), 
   aes(x=plate_ID, y=proportion, fill=phylum)) + 
   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") + 
   facet_grid(cols = vars(groups2), scales = "free_x") + 
   labs(x = NULL, y = "Relative Abundance (%)", fill = "Genus") + #"Baseline", 
   theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
         axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), 
         panel.background = element_blank(), 
         plot.background = element_blank(), 
         panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
         panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
         strip.background = element_blank(), 
         legend.position = "none") + 
     theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 25))

p2 <- ggplot(
  phylum_long %>% filter(collection_part == "Follow Up"), 
  aes(x=plate_ID, y=proportion, fill=phylum)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") + 
  facet_grid(cols = vars(groups2), scales = "free_x") + 
  labs(x = "Technical Replicates (n=3) per Collection Point", #NULL,#"Follow Up", 
       y = "Relative Abundance (%)", fill = "Genus") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), 
        panel.background = element_blank(), 
        plot.background = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
        strip.text.x = element_blank(), 
        strip.background = element_blank(), 
        legend.position = "none") + 
  theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 5, b = 5, l = 25))


# Remove y-axis labels from individual plots
p1_clean <- p1 + theme(
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.title.y = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 7)
)
p2_clean <- p2 + theme(
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.title.y = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 7)
)

# Stack the two plots vertically
plots <- plot_grid(
  p1_clean, p2_clean,
  ncol = 1,
  align = "v"
)

# Add shared right-side labels for Baseline / Follow Up
ylabels_right <- plot_grid(
  ggdraw() + draw_label("Baseline", angle = -90, x = 0.05, y = 0.5, size = 10),
  ggdraw() + draw_label("Follow Up", angle = -90, x = 0.05, y = 0.5, size = 10),
  ncol = 1
)

# Add shared y-axis label on the left
shared_ylabel <- ggdraw() + draw_label(
  "Relative Abundance (%)", angle = 90, x = 0.5, y = 0.5, size = 10
)

# Combine left y-label, stacked plots, and right labels
main <- plot_grid(
  shared_ylabel, plots, ylabels_right,
  ncol = 3,
  rel_widths = c(0.01, 1, 0.01)  # left label narrow, plots wide, right labels narrow
)

# Shared legend
legend_plot <- ggplot(phylum_long, aes(x = plate_ID, y = proportion, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  labs(fill = "Genus") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "lines")
  )
shared_legend <- get_legend(legend_plot)

# Add shared legend to the far right
rel_abund.plot <- plot_grid(
  main, shared_legend,
  ncol = 2,
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.31)
)

# Display final plot
rel_abund.plot


```

```{r Bray Curtis Plot}

## Bray Curtis ####

##Calculate Bray-Curtis Distance Matrix
tech.dist_mat <- metaphlan4_all %>% 
  #select tehcnical Controls
  filter(plate_ID %in% technical_ids) %>% 
  #select species level data
  filter(rank %in% c("species")) %>% #,"genus","subspecies")) %>%
  #pivot to wider form
  pivot_wider(., 
    id_cols=c(plate_ID), #sample stays as row
    names_from=name, #name becomes column with rel abun as value
    values_from=relative_abundance,
    values_fill = 0) %>%  #covert NA relative abundance to 0
  #bray curtis prep
  column_to_rownames("plate_ID") %>% #turning column values to name
  as.matrix() %>% #convert to matrix
  vegdist(method = "bray")

##Extract Within-Sample Distances
#convert distance object to long format
tech.dist_df <- as.matrix(tech.dist_mat) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("sample1") %>%
  pivot_longer(-sample1, names_to = "sample2", values_to = "bray_curtis") %>%
  filter(sample1 != sample2)  # remove self-comparisons
#extract a common sample group identifier (adjust pattern as needed)
tech.dist_df <- tech.dist_df %>%
  mutate(
    sample_group1 = case_when(
      sample1 %in% c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408") ~ "012_P1", #plate1 012_T1-T3
      sample1 %in% c("GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807") ~ "079_P1", #plate2 079_T1-T3
      sample1 %in% c("GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120") ~ "023_P2", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
      sample1 %in% c("GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415") ~ "012_P2", #plate4 012_T4-T6
      sample1 %in% c("GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402") ~ "079_P2", #plate4 079_T4-T6
      sample1 %in% c("GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") ~ "023_P1", #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)
      TRUE ~ NA ),
    sample_group2 = case_when(
      sample2 %in% c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408") ~ "012_P1", #plate1 012_T1-T3
      sample2 %in% c("GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807") ~ "079_P1", #plate2 079_T1-T3
      sample2 %in% c("GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120") ~ "023_P2", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
      sample2 %in% c("GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415") ~ "012_P2", #plate4 012_T4-T6
      sample2 %in% c("GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402") ~ "079_P2", #plate4 079_T4-T6
      sample2 %in% c("GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") ~ "023_P1", #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)
      TRUE ~ NA )#
  ) %>% 
  filter(sample_group1 == sample_group2)
#summarize within each technical replicate group
tech.dist_df <- tech.dist_df %>%
  filter(sample1 < sample2) %>%
  group_by(sample_group1) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_bc = mean(bray_curtis),
    sd_bc = sd(bray_curtis),
    max_bc = max(bray_curtis),
    n_pairs = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_bc)) %>%
  mutate(label = glue("Bray-Curtis dist: {round(mean_bc, 3)} (±{round(sd_bc, 3)})"))


## Calculation PCOA

# Calculating PCOAs
beta_pcoa <- cmdscale(tech.dist_mat, #pcoa is list with $points (PCs per sample)
                   k = 2, #N-1 PCs (default 2)
                   eig = TRUE, # $eig (explained variation), $x (null), $ac, $GOF
                   add = TRUE) #added add=T from Sandeep
#turn pcoa points from list to dataframe
beta_pcoa.df <- as.data.frame(beta_pcoa$points) %>% #turn list component to dataframe
  rownames_to_column(var = "plate_ID") %>% #study id stored as row_name
  rename_with(~paste0("PC", gsub("V", "", .)), starts_with("V")) %>% #col names from V to PC
  #creating grouping var
  mutate(
    groups = case_when(
      plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00307","GMBC_00412","GMBC_00408") ~ "012_P1", #plate1 012_T1-T3
      plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_00617","GMBC_00911","GMBC_00807") ~ "079_P1", #plate2 079_T1-T3
      plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01204","GMBC_01216","GMBC_01120") ~ "023_P2", #plate3 023_T1-T3 (part 2)
      plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01401","GMBC_01315","GMBC_01415") ~ "012_P2", #plate4 012_T4-T6
      plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01403","GMBC_01310","GMBC_01402") ~ "079_P2", #plate4 079_T4-T6
      plate_ID %in% c("GMBC_01409","GMBC_01314","GMBC_01407") ~ "023_P1", #plate4 023_T4-T6.P1 (part 1)
      TRUE ~ NA ),
    collection_part = ifelse(str_detect(groups, "P1"), 1, 2),
    groups2 = case_when (
            str_detect(groups, "012") ~ "012",
            str_detect(groups, "079") ~ "079",
            str_detect(groups, "023") ~ "023"),
    groups2 = factor(groups2, levels = c("012","023","079"),
                     labels = c("Participant 12","Participant 23","Participant 79")),
    collection_part = factor(collection_part, levels = c("1","2"),
                             labels = c("Baseline", "Follow Up"))
  )



# Join BC distance labels to centroid positions
centroid_labels <- beta_pcoa.df %>%
  group_by(groups = groups) %>%  # Adjust if your grouping var is named differently
  summarize(
    mean_PC1 = mean(PC1, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_PC2 = mean(PC2, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  left_join(tech.dist_df, by = c("groups" = "sample_group1")) %>% 
  mutate(
    mean_PC1 = case_when(
      groups=="012_P1" ~ -0.18,
      groups=="012_P2" ~ -0.28,
      groups=="079_P1" ~  -0.03,
      groups=="079_P2" ~  0.00,
      groups=="023_P1" ~ -0.09,
      groups=="023_P2" ~ -0.19,
      TRUE ~ mean_PC1
    ),
    mean_PC2 = case_when(
      groups=="012_P1" ~ 0.24,
      groups=="012_P2" ~ 0.285,
      groups=="079_P1" ~ 0.05,
      groups=="079_P2" ~ -0.01,
      groups=="023_P1" ~ -0.32,
      groups=="023_P2" ~ -0.285,
      TRUE ~ mean_PC2
    )
  )


## Plotting PCOAs
beta_plot <- beta_pcoa.df %>% 
  filter(!is.na(plate_ID)) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = groups2, shape = collection_part)) + 
  geom_point(size=4) +
  labs(x = glue("PCoA 1"),
       y = glue("PCoA 2"),
       color = "Participant",
       shape = "Collection Point") + 
  theme(#legend.position = "none",
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.background = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
        panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines"),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = c(0.2, 0.5),    # inside top right (x,y in npc)
        #legend.box = "horizontal",
        #legend.justification = c("left", "top"),
        legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white"),
        legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "lines"),
        #legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "cm"), 
        #legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "cm"), 
       ) +
       guides(
         color = guide_legend(order=1),
         shape = guide_legend(order=2)
         ) # make it compact


final.beta_plot <- beta_plot +
    geom_text(
    data = centroid_labels,
    aes(x = mean_PC1, y = mean_PC2, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,  # 👈 prevents using aes from main ggplot
    color = "black", size = 3, hjust = 0, vjust = -0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) 



```
 
```{r quality figs - combined plot }

library(cowplot)

png("metaphlan_quality_2025-08-26.png", 
    width = 9 * 600, height = 3.5 * 600, 
    res = 600, bg = "white")

print(
  # Arrange side-by-side with custom widths
  plot_grid(
    final.beta_plot,
    rel_abund.plot,
    nrow = 1,
    rel_widths = c(1, 1.31),
    labels = c("A", "B"),           # built-in labels
    label_size = 15,
    label_fontface = "bold",
    label_x = 0.01,                 # fine-tune horizontal offset
    label_y = 0.99                  # fine-tune vertical offset
  )
)

dev.off()




```


## Summary
  
Taxonomic Abundance (MetaPhlAn4)
```{r metaphlan summary and estrobolome targets}


## PRE-Filtering Unique Species and Prevalent Phylums

#overall (pre filtering) - after removal of controls
metaphlan4 %>% filter(rank == "genus") %>% distinct(name) %>% nrow()
metaphlan4 %>% filter(rank == "species") %>% distinct(name) %>% nrow()
metaphlan4 %>% filter(rank == "subspecies") %>% distinct(name) %>% nrow()

#kingdom level data - summing relative abundances for each kingdom across all samples
metaphlan4 %>%
  filter(rank == "phylum") %>% 
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarise(total_abundance = sum(relative_abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(proportion = total_abundance / sum(total_abundance))


## POST-Filtering Unique and Prevalent Species (Robertson 2023)

uni <- metaphlan4_long %>% filter(rank=="genus") %>% distinct(name)
glue("The number of unique genera in P1 samples after filtering: {nrow(uni)}")
uni.1 <- uni %>% distinct() %>% filter(grepl("GGB",name))
glue("Of these, {nrow(uni.1)} included GGB genera ({round(nrow(uni.1)/nrow(uni),3)*100}%)")

uni.species <- metaphlan4_long %>% filter(rank == "species") %>% distinct(name)
glue("The number of unique species in P1 samples after filtering: {nrow(uni.species)}")
uni.species1 <- uni.species %>% distinct() %>% filter(grepl("GGB",name))
glue("Of these, {nrow(uni.species1)} included GGB_SGB species ({round(nrow(uni.species1)/nrow(uni.species),3)*100}%)")

metaphlan4_long %>% filter(rank=="subspecies") %>% distinct(name) %>%  nrow()

#kingdom level data - summing relative abundances for each kingdom across all samples
metaphlan4_long %>%
  filter(rank == "phylum") %>% 
  group_by(name) %>%
  summarise(total_abundance = sum(relative_abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(proportion = total_abundance / sum(total_abundance))

#F/B Ratio by case_con status
metaphlan4_long %>%
  filter(rank == "phylum") %>%
  merge(gmbc_data %>% select(plate_ID, case_con), by = "plate_ID", all.x = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(case_con, name) %>%
  summarise(total_abundance = sum(relative_abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = total_abundance) %>%
  mutate(FB_ratio = `p__Firmicutes` / `p__Bacteroidetes`) %>% 
  t() %>% 
  view()


#predominant species - sum relabun for each species then sort desc
metaphlan4_wide.s %>% 
  select(!plate_ID) %>% 
  colSums() %>% 
  sort(decreasing = TRUE) %>% 
  head(5)


### RUN THE SUMMARY TABLE
describe_data(metaphlan4_wide, "plate_ID","metaphlan")

### Estrobolome Target Coverage

glue("The number of genera-level estrobolome targets: {nrow(estrobolome_targets %>% filter(rank=='genus'))}")
glue("The number of species-level estrobolome targets: {nrow(estrobolome_targets %>% filter(rank=='species'))}")

#PRIOR to data filtering
estrobolome_prefilt <- metaphlan4 %>%  
  filter(name %in% estrobolome_targets$metaphlan_GMBC) %>%
  distinct(name)

#found
glue("Genera targets found prior to filtering: {nrow(estrobolome_prefilt %>% filter(grepl('g__', name)))}")
glue("Species targets found prior to filtering: {nrow(estrobolome_prefilt %>% filter(grepl('s__', name)))}")
#not found in metaphlan
estrobolome_targets %>% filter(rank=="genus") %>% filter(!metaphlan_GMBC %in% estrobolome_prefilt$name) %>% pull(name)
estrobolome_targets %>% filter(rank=="species") %>% filter(!metaphlan_GMBC %in% estrobolome_prefilt$name) %>% pull(name)


#POST data filtering
estrobolome_postfilt <- metaphlan4_long %>%
  filter(name %in% estrobolome_targets$metaphlan_GMBC) %>%
  distinct(name)

#remaining
glue("Genera targets remaining after filtering: {nrow(estrobolome_postfilt %>% filter(grepl('g__', name)))}")
glue("Species targets remaining after filtering): {nrow(estrobolome_postfilt %>% filter(grepl('s__', name)))}")
#removed
estrobolome_prefilt %>% filter(grepl('g__', name)) %>% filter(!name %in% estrobolome_postfilt$name) %>% pull(name)
estrobolome_prefilt %>% filter(grepl('s__', name)) %>% filter(!name %in% estrobolome_postfilt$name)%>% pull(name)


```

Pathway Abundance (HUMAnN3)
```{r humann.path summary}

## RAW DATA DESCRIPTION
glue("The max relabun raw data: {max(humann.path_nopipe$relative_abundance)} (unintegrated)")
glue("The min relabun raw data: {min(humann.path_nopipe$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The max relabun raw data (removing unmapped, unintegrated): {
     humann.path_nopipe %>% filter(!name %in% c('UNINTEGRATED','UNMAPPED')) %>% pull(relative_abundance) %>% max()}")
glue("The avg relabun for unintegrated data (raw): {
     mean(humann.path_nopipe %>% filter(name == 'UNINTEGRATED') %>% pull(relative_abundance)) }")
glue("The avg relabun for unmapped data (raw): {
     mean(humann.path_nopipe %>% filter(name == 'UNMAPPED') %>% pull(relative_abundance)) }")
glue("The number of unique pathways in data (raw): {nrow( humann.path_nopipe %>% distinct(name) ) }")


## FILTERED DATA
glue("The max relabun filtered data: {max(humann.path_long$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The min relabun filtered data: {min(humann.path_long$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The number of unique pathways in filtered data: {nrow( humann.path_long %>% distinct(name) ) }")

#predominant pathways - sum relabun for each path then sort desc
humann.path_wide %>% 
  select(!plate_ID) %>% 
  colSums() %>% 
  sort(decreasing = TRUE) %>% 
  head(5)


### RUN THE SUMMARY TABLE
describe_data(humann.path_wide, "plate_ID","humann3_path")

## Estrobolome Targets

#overall number
glue("The number of pathway estrobolome targets: {nrow(estrobolome_targets %>% filter(!is.na(Pathway_code)))}")

#found in raw data
estrobolome.path_prefilt <- humann.path_nopipe %>%
  filter(sapply(name, function(x) any(str_detect(x, estrobolome_targets$Pathway_code)))) %>%
  distinct(name) %>% 
  filter(!grepl("GALACT", name))
estrobolome.path_prefilt$name

#found in filtered p1 data
estrobolome.path_postfilt <- humann.path_long %>%
  filter(sapply(name, function(x) any(str_detect(x, estrobolome_targets$Pathway_code)))) %>%
  distinct(name) %>% 
  filter(!grepl("GALACT", name))
estrobolome.path_postfilt$name

#those not found
estrobolome_targets %>% 
    filter(!sapply(Pathway_code, function(x) any(str_detect(x, estrobolome.path_prefilt$name)))) %>%
    distinct(Pathway_code, Pathway_name)

``` 

Enzyme Abundance (HUMAnN3)
```{r humann.enzymes summary}

## RAW DATA DESCRIPTION

glue("The max relabun raw data: {max(humann.enzymes_nopipe$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The min relabun raw data: {min(humann.enzymes_nopipe$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The number of unique enzymes in raw samples: {nrow( humann.enzymes_nopipe %>% distinct(name) ) }")

## FILTERED DATA
glue("The max relabun filtered data: {max(humann.enzymes_long$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The min relabun filtered data: {min(humann.enzymes_long$relative_abundance)}")
glue("The number of unique enzymes in filtered data: {nrow(humann.enzymes_long %>% distinct(name))}")

#predominant enzymes - sum relabun for each path then sort desc
humann.enzymes_wide %>% 
  select(!plate_ID) %>% 
  colSums() %>% 
  sort(decreasing = TRUE) %>% 
  head(5) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  rownames_to_column("name") %>% 
  merge(., humann.enzymes_nopipe %>% select(name, Enzyme_name),
        by = "name") %>% 
  unique() %>% 
  mutate(names = paste0(Enzyme_name," (", name, ")")) %>% 
  select(-name, -Enzyme_name)

### RUN THE SUMMARY TABLE
describe_data(humann.enzymes_wide, "plate_ID","humann3_enzymes")

## Estrobolome Targets

#overall number
glue("The number of enzyme estrobolome targets: {nrow(estrobolome_targets %>% filter(!is.na(Enzyme_code)))}")

#found in raw p1 data
estrobolome.enzyme_prefilt <- humann.enzymes_nopipe %>%
  filter(name %in% estrobolome_targets$Enzyme_code) %>%
  distinct(name, Enzyme_name)
glue("The number of estrobolome enzyme targets found in baseline raw data: {nrow(estrobolome.enzyme_prefilt)}")
#those found
estrobolome.enzyme_prefilt %>% distinct(Enzyme_name,name) %>% pull(Enzyme_name, name)
#those not found
estrobolome_targets %>% filter(!Enzyme_code %in% estrobolome.enzyme_prefilt$name) %>% distinct(Enzyme_name) %>% pull(Enzyme_name)


#found in filtered p1 data
estrobolome.enzyme_postfilt <- humann.enzymes_long %>%
  filter(name %in% estrobolome_targets$Enzyme_code) %>%
  distinct(name)
glue("The number of estrobolome enzyme targets found in baseline filtered data: {nrow(estrobolome.enzyme_postfilt)}")
#not found
setdiff(estrobolome.enzyme_prefilt$name, estrobolome.enzyme_postfilt$name)

```

 
## Analyses

Alpha and Beta Diversity 
```{r alpha and beta diversity - apply metaphlan}

#run alpha diversity
alpha_results <- run_alpha_diversity(metaphlan4_long, metaphlan=TRUE)

#plot alpha and beta diversity together
final_plot <- plot_alpha_beta(
  alpha_results$alpha_data, 
  #run beta diversity and get plot
  beta_plot = plot_beta_diversity(metaphlan4_wide.t, -0.43, -0.35),
  #plot alpha diversity - shannon
  alpha_plot1 = plot_alpha_diversity(alpha_results$alpha_data, shannon, "Shannon Index",
                                     alpha_results$p_shannon, 1.5, 2.5),
  #plot alpha diversity - richness
  alpha_plot2 = plot_alpha_diversity(alpha_results$alpha_data, richness, "Species Richness",
                                     alpha_results$p_richness, 1.5, 60),
  #"A","B","C",
  "","","",
  "alpha-beta_metaphlan_2025-08-18.png"
)


```

```{r alpha and beta diversity - apply humann.path}

#run alpha diversity
alpha_results <- run_alpha_diversity(humann.path_long)

#plot alpha and beta diversity together
final_plot.path <- plot_alpha_beta(
  alpha_results$alpha_data, 
  #run beta diversity and get plot
  beta_plot = plot_beta_diversity(humann.path_wide, 0.1, 0.11),
  #plot alpha diversity - shannon
  alpha_plot1 = plot_alpha_diversity(alpha_results$alpha_data, shannon, "Shannon Index",
                                     alpha_results$p_shannon, 1.5, 5.55),
  #plot alpha diversity - richness
  alpha_plot2 = plot_alpha_diversity(alpha_results$alpha_data, richness, "Pathway Richness",
                                     alpha_results$p_richness, 1.5, 410),
  #"A","B","C",
  "","","",
  "alpha-beta_humann.path_2025-08-18.png"
)

```

```{r alpha and beta diversity - apply humann.enzymes}

#run alpha diversity
alpha_results <- run_alpha_diversity(humann.enzymes_long)

#plot alpha and beta diversity together
final_plot.enzymes <- plot_alpha_beta(
  alpha_results$alpha_data, 
  #run beta diversity and get plot
  beta_plot = plot_beta_diversity(humann.enzymes_wide, 0.07, 0.16),
  #plot alpha diversity - shannon
  alpha_plot1 = plot_alpha_diversity(alpha_results$alpha_data, shannon, "Shannon Index",
                                     alpha_results$p_shannon, 1.5, 6.59),
  #plot alpha diversity - richness
  alpha_plot2 = plot_alpha_diversity(alpha_results$alpha_data, richness, "Enzyme Richness",
                                     alpha_results$p_richness, 1.5, 1850),
  #"D","E","F",
  "","","",
  "alpha-beta_humann.enzymes_2025-08-18.png"
)

```

```{r alpha/beta combined path + enzymes plot }

library(magick)

# Read the two PNG files
img0 <- image_read("alpha-beta_metaphlan_2025-08-18.png")
img1 <- image_read("alpha-beta_humann.path_2025-08-18.png")
img2 <- image_read("alpha-beta_humann.enzymes_2025-08-18.png")

# Combine side by side
combined <- image_append(c(img0, img1, img2), stack=TRUE)  # horizontal by default

# Save the combined image
image_write(combined, "combined_plot.png")




```

```{r Combined plot}

library(cowplot)

png("diversity_2025-11-13.png", 
    width = 6.5 * 600, height = 6.5 * 600, 
    res = 600, bg = "white")

# Arrange side-by-side with custom widths
print(
  plot_grid(
    #row 1, metaphlan
    plot_grid(
      plot_grid(metaphlan.shannon, metaphlan.richness, nrow = 2, rel_heights = c(1, 1)),
      metaphlan.beta_plot,
      nrow = 1,
      ncol = 2,
      rel_widths = c(1,1.5)
    ),
    #row 2, pathways
    plot_grid(
      plot_grid(path.shannon, path.richness, nrow = 2, rel_heights = c(1, 1)),
      path.beta_plot,
      nrow = 1,
      ncol = 2,
      rel_widths = c(1,1.5)
    ),
    #arguments
    nrow = 2,
    ncol = 2,
    rel_heights = c(1,1)
  )
)



grid.text("Species", x = unit(0.45, "npc"), y = unit(0.985, "npc"), #0.099 x
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("Pathways", x = unit(0.45, "npc"), y = unit(0.675, "npc"), #0.348
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("Enzymes", x = unit(0.45, "npc"), y = unit(0.342, "npc"), 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("Cases", x = unit(0.15, "npc"), y = unit(0.993, "npc"), #0.099 x
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 9))

grid.text("Controls", x = unit(0.305, "npc"), y = unit(0.993, "npc"), #0.099 x
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 9))


dev.off()


```


Estrobolome - Regression Modelling
```{r LogReg - estrobolome targets all}

## Combine all estrobolome targets datasets
#target list
estrobolome_list <- c(estrobolome_targets$metaphlan_GMBC,
                      estrobolome_targets$Enzyme_code,
                      estrobolome_targets$Pathway_code)
estrobolome_list <- Filter(Negate(is.na), estrobolome_list)
#select from each datatype
estro.t <- metaphlan4_wide.t %>% 
  transform_metagenomics(merge=FALSE) %>% 
  select(plate_ID, matches(paste(estrobolome_list, collapse = "|")))
estro.g <- metaphlan4_wide.g %>% 
  transform_metagenomics(merge=FALSE) %>% 
  select(plate_ID, matches(paste(estrobolome_list, collapse = "|")))
estro.p <- humann.path_wide %>% 
  transform_metagenomics(merge=FALSE) %>% 
  select(plate_ID, matches(paste(estrobolome_list, collapse = "|"))) %>% 
  select(!contains("GALACT"))
estro.e <- humann.enzymes_wide %>% 
  transform_metagenomics() %>% 
  select(plate_ID, matches(paste(estrobolome_list, collapse = "|")),
         case_con, "s_age","s_bmi","ethnic_collapsed","d_education_collapsed",
    "hormone_current.p1","sm_summary","pa_cat","Alcohol.g",
    "family_history","health_condition","rh_pregnancy_live","rh_pregnancy_age_first_cat",
    "rh_breastfeed_months","rh_contraceptive","hormone_ever",
    "Total.dietary.fibre.g_density",
    "Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g_density",
    "Total.sugar.g_density" )
#merge all together
estro.full <- estro.t %>% 
  left_join(estro.g, by="plate_ID") %>% 
  left_join(estro.p, by="plate_ID") %>% 
  left_join(estro.e, by="plate_ID")


#run: iterative modeling - apply
apply_iterative.modeling(
  predictor_vars <- estro.full %>% select(2:72) %>% colnames(), 
  adjustment_sets <- get_covariates(),
  dataset.model <- estro.full,
  file_name <- "estrobolome.targets_clr"
)

```

```{r LogReg unadjusted - estrobolome targets all volcano plot}

library(readxl)

## UNADJUSTED
results <- read_excel("Logreg_estrobolome.targets_clr_2025-09-05.xlsx", sheet = "Sheet_unadj") %>%
  mutate(
    sig = ifelse(q.value < 0.25, "Yes (q<0.25)", "No (q≥0.25)"), # & p.value < 0.05
    log10_FDR = -log10(q.value)
  )

## SCATTER WITH BAR PLOT

# Step 1: Generate histogram data from the OR column
hist_data <- ggplot_build(
  ggplot(results, aes(x = OR)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 100)
)$data[[1]]

# Step 2: Rescale histogram y values to negative
hist_data$y <- -hist_data$count / max(hist_data$count) / 8  # adjust height if needed

# Step 3: Final plot
volcano.e.0 <- ggplot() +
  # Histogram bars below y = 0
  geom_col(data = hist_data, aes(x = x, y = y), fill = "black", width = hist_data$width[1]) +
  
  # Scatter points
  geom_point(data = results, aes(x = OR, y = log10_FDR, shape = shape, color = sig),
             alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +

  # Scales and lines
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Yes (q<0.25)" = "red", "No (q≥0.25)" = "grey70")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Species" = 16,
                                 "Genus" = 17,
                                 "Pathway" = 4,
                                 "Enzyme" = 15)) +
  #geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 1, y = 0, yend=1.26),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.25), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black", alpha=1) +

  # Adjust y-axis to make room for bars
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.07, NA)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.75, 1.25)) +
  #scale_x_continuous(limits = c(NA, 1.5)) +
  labs(
    x = "Odds Ratio",
    y = "-log10(FDR)",
    color = "Significance",
    shape = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

volcano.e.0
```
  
```{r LogReg adjusted - estrobolome targets all volcano plot}

library(readxl)

## CONFOUNDER
results <-  read_excel("Logreg_estrobolome.targets_clr_2025-09-05.xlsx", sheet = "Sheet_confounder_vars") %>%
  mutate(
    sig = ifelse(q.value < 0.25, "Yes (q<0.25)", "No (q≥0.25)"),
    log10_FDR = -log10(q.value)
  ) 

#duplicate row 1, give big OR so outside of range, make POS for the LEGEND
results <- rbind(results, results[1, ])
results[nrow(results), "OR"] <- 5
results[nrow(results), "sig"] <- "Yes (q<0.25)"


## SCATTER WITH BAR PLOT

# Step 1: Generate histogram data from the OR column
hist_data <- ggplot_build(
  ggplot(results, aes(x = OR)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 250)
)$data[[1]]

# Step 2: Rescale histogram y values to negative
hist_data$y <- -hist_data$count / max(hist_data$count) / 8  # adjust height if needed

# Step 3: Final plot
volcano.e.1 <- ggplot() +
  # Histogram bars below y = 0
  geom_col(data = hist_data, aes(x = x, y = y), fill = "black", width = hist_data$width[1]) +
  
  # Scatter points
  geom_point(data = results, aes(x = OR, y = log10_FDR, shape = shape, color = sig),
             alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +

  # Scales and lines
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Yes (q<0.25)" = "red", "No (q≥0.25)" = "grey70")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("Species" = 16,
                                 "Genus" = 17,
                                 "Pathway" = 4,
                                 "Enzyme" = 15)) +
  #geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(x = 1, xend = 1, y = 0, yend=1.26),
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.25), linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black", alpha=1) +

  # Adjust y-axis to make room for bars
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.07, NA)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.75, 1.25)) +
  #scale_x_continuous(limits = c(NA, 1.5)) +
  labs(
    x = "Odds Ratio",
    y = NULL,#"-log10(FDR)",
    color = "Significant",
    shape = "Feature"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6.8),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = c(1, 0.98),    # inside top right (x,y in npc)
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.justification = c("right", "top"),
    legend.background = element_rect(color = "black", fill = "white"),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.7, "lines"),
    #legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, "cm"), 
    #legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "cm"), 
   ) +
   guides(
     color = guide_legend(order=1),
     shape = guide_legend(ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE, order=2)
     ) # make it compact

volcano.e.1
```

```{r LogReg - estrobolome combine volcano plot }

library(cowplot)

png("volcano_estrobolome_2025-09-05.png", 
    width = 6.5 * 600, height = 3.25 * 600, 
    res = 600, bg = "white")

# Arrange side-by-side with custom widths
print(
  plot_grid(
    volcano.e.0, volcano.e.1,
    nrow = 1,
    rel_widths = c(1, 1)
  )
)

grid.text("A", x = unit(0.08, "npc"), y = unit(0.94, "npc"), 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))

grid.text("B", x = unit(0.55, "npc"), y = unit(0.94, "npc"), 
          gp = gpar(fontsize = 15, fontface = "bold"))

dev.off()


```


XGBoost Top Features - Regression Modelling
```{r LogReg - XGBoost subset}

## Combine selected features and datasets
#target list
estrobolome_list <- c(
  "g__Blautia",
  "g__Alistipes",
  "g__Hungatella",
  "g__Candidatus_Avimonas",
  "g__GGB3057",
  "g__Faecalicatena",
  "g__GGB79634",
  "g__Christensenellaceae_unclassified",
  "g__GGB13404",
  "g__Intestinimonas",
  "g__GGB79734",
  "g__GGB9063",
  "g__Lachnoclostridium",
  "g__GGB9635",
  "g__GGB80140",
  "s__Anaerotruncus_rubiinfantis|t__SGB25416",
  "s__Alistipes_shahii|t__SGB2295",
  "s__Lachnospiraceae_bacterium_AM48_27BH|t__SGB4706",
  "s__Alistipes_finegoldii|t__SGB2301",
  "s__Blautia_obeum|t__SGB4811",
  "s__Sutterella_wadsworthensis|t__SGB9286",
  "s__Alistipes_putredinis|t__SGB2318",
  "s__Mediterraneibacter_massiliensis|t__SGB4595",
  "s__Candidatus_Avimonas_narfia|t__SGB14941",
  "s__Mediterraneibacter_butyricigenes|t__SGB25493",
  "s__Blautia_SGB4815|t__SGB4815",
  "s__Faecalibacillus_intestinalis|t__SGB6754",
  "s__Clostridium_fessum|t__SGB4705",
  "s__Candidatus_Cibionibacter_quicibialis|t__SGB15286",
  "s__Anaerobutyricum_hallii|t__SGB4532",
  "s__GGB3057_SGB4059|t__SGB4059",
  "PWY-7992: superpathway of menaquinol-8 biosynthesis III",
  "PWY-7992: superpathway of menaquinol-8 biosynthesis III",
  "RIBOSYN2-PWY: flavin biosynthesis I (bacteria and plants)",
  "PWY-7883: anhydromuropeptides recycling II",
  "PWY-5130: 2-oxobutanoate degradation I",
  "PWY-7761: NAD salvage pathway II (PNC IV cycle)",
  "PWY-6876: isopropanol biosynthesis (engineered)",
  "PWY-6901: superpathway of glucose and xylose degradation",
  "P42-PWY: incomplete reductive TCA cycle",
  "PWY-8131: 5'-deoxyadenosine degradation II",
  "METHGLYUT-PWY: superpathway of methylglyoxal degradation",
  "PWY-7013: (S)-propane-1,2-diol degradation",
  "PWY-6588: pyruvate fermentation to acetone",
  "PWY-7371: 1,4-dihydroxy-6-naphthoate biosynthesis II",
  "PWY-5973: cis-vaccenate biosynthesis",
  "PWY-5920: superpathway of heme b biosynthesis from glycine",
  "1.8.98.1",
  "3.5.1.81",
  "2.7.1.113",
  "2.5.1.120",
  "3.4.13.21",
  "1.2.99.5",
  "6.3.4.15",
  "1.4.99.6",
  "3.4.22.8",
  "4.2.1.115",
  "2.4.1.319",
  "2.4.1.329",
  "2.1.1.173",
  "1.10.9.1",
  "5.1.3.8",
  "2.1.1.61"
)

#select from each datatype
estro.t <- metaphlan4_wide.g %>% #t
  transform_metagenomics() %>% #merge=FALSE
  select(plate_ID, all_of(intersect(estrobolome_list, colnames(.)))) 
estro.p <- humann.path_wide %>%
  transform_metagenomics(merge=FALSE) %>%
  select(plate_ID, all_of(intersect(estrobolome_list, colnames(.))))
estro.e <- humann.enzymes_wide %>%
  transform_metagenomics() %>%
  select(plate_ID, all_of(intersect(estrobolome_list, colnames(.))),
         case_con, "s_age","s_bmi","ethnic_collapsed","d_education_collapsed",
    "hormone_current.p1","sm_summary","pa_cat","Alcohol.g",
    "Total.dietary.fibre.g_density",
    "Total.saturated.fatty.acids.g_density",
    "Total.sugar.g_density"
)
#merge all together
estro.full <- estro.t %>%
  left_join(estro.p, by="plate_ID") %>%
  left_join(estro.e, by="plate_ID")


#run: iterative modeling - apply
apply_iterative.modeling(
  predictor_vars <- estrobolome_list, 
  adjustment_sets <- get_covariates(),
  dataset.model <- estro.full,
  file_name <- "top.features_clr"
)

```


XGBoost Top Features - Relative Abundance Plots       
```{r genus - relabun plot}

## top 15 features list
features <- c(
  "g__Blautia",
  "g__Alistipes",
  "g__Hungatella",
  "g__Candidatus_Avimonas",
  "g__GGB3057",
  "g__Faecalicatena",
  "g__GGB79634",
  "g__Christensenellaceae_unclassified",
  "g__GGB13404",
  "g__Intestinimonas",
  "g__GGB79734",
  "g__GGB9063",
  "g__Lachnoclostridium",
  "g__GGB9635",
  "g__GGB80140"
)

## Metaphlan data
maaslin2_data.plot <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  filter(name %in% features) %>% 
  select(plate_ID, name, relative_abundance) %>% 
  merge(., gmbc_data %>% select(plate_ID, single_id, collection_part, case_con)) %>% 
  mutate(case_con = factor(case_con, 
                           levels = c(0, 1), 
                           labels = c('Control', 'Case'))) %>% 
  #clean up names
  mutate(
    name = name %>%
      str_remove("^g__") %>%         # remove leading s__
      str_replace_all("_", " ")       # replace underscores with spaces
  ) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Christensenellaceae unclassified", 
                       "Christensenell. uncl.", name)) %>% 
  #filter out zero vals
  filter(relative_abundance > 0) %>% 
  #log transform for visualization on plot
  mutate(log_rel_abundance = log10(relative_abundance + 1e-6))
  

## Clean up features list the same way as in the data
features_clean <- features %>%
  str_remove("^g__") %>%
  str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
  ifelse(. == "Christensenellaceae unclassified", "Christensenell. uncl.", .)

## Apply the factor ordering
maaslin2_data.plot <- maaslin2_data.plot %>%
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = rev(features_clean)))  # rev() = top-to-bottom order


## Plotting
rel_abun.genus <- ggplot(maaslin2_data.plot, aes(y=name, x=log_rel_abundance, color=case_con)) +
  #adding in the individual rel_abun points
  geom_jitter(position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.8,
                                              jitter.width = 0.1),
              shape=21, alpha = 0.12) +
  #boxplots per group per species
  geom_boxplot(aes(group = interaction(name, case_con), fill = case_con),
               position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
               alpha = 0.4,
               outlier.shape = NA) +
  labs(y=NULL, x="log10(Relative Abundance)") +
  #manually setting colours of the groups
  scale_color_manual(name = NULL, values = c("Case" = "blue4", "Control" = "grey30")) +
  scale_fill_manual(name = NULL, values = c("Case" = "blue4", "Control" = "grey30")) +
  #overall theme
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(), #element_markdown(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
        legend.position = "none"
        # legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        # legend.position = c(0.92, 0.5),
        # legend.background = element_rect(color="black", fill = NA),
        # legend.margin = margin(t=1, r=3, b=3),
        # legend.key.size = unit(0.7, "lines"),
        )


```
  
```{r species - relabun plot}

## top 15 features list
features <- c(
  "s__Anaerotruncus_rubiinfantis|t__SGB25416",
  "s__Alistipes_shahii|t__SGB2295",
  "s__Lachnospiraceae_bacterium_AM48_27BH|t__SGB4706",
  "s__Alistipes_finegoldii|t__SGB2301",
  "s__Blautia_obeum|t__SGB4811",
  "s__Sutterella_wadsworthensis|t__SGB9286",
  "s__Alistipes_putredinis|t__SGB2318",
  "s__Mediterraneibacter_massiliensis|t__SGB4595",
  "s__Candidatus_Avimonas_narfia|t__SGB14941",
  "s__Mediterraneibacter_butyricigenes|t__SGB25493",
  "s__Blautia_SGB4815|t__SGB4815",
  "s__Faecalibacillus_intestinalis|t__SGB6754",
  "s__Clostridium_fessum|t__SGB4705",
  "s__Candidatus_Cibionibacter_quicibialis|t__SGB15286",
  "s__Anaerobutyricum_hallii|t__SGB4532"
)

## Metaphlan data
maaslin2_data.plot <- metaphlan4_long %>% 
  filter(name %in% features) %>% 
  select(plate_ID, name, relative_abundance) %>% 
  merge(., gmbc_data %>% select(plate_ID, single_id, collection_part, case_con)) %>% 
  mutate(case_con = factor(case_con, 
                           levels = c(0, 1), 
                           labels = c('Control', 'Case'))) %>% 
  #clean up names
  mutate(
    name = name %>%
      str_remove("^s__") %>%                   # remove leading s__
      str_remove("\\|t__SGB[0-9]+") %>%        # remove "|t__SGB####"
      str_replace_all("_", " ")                # replace underscores with spaces
  ) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Lachnospiraceae bacterium AM48 27BH", 
                       "L. bact. AM48-27BH", name)) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Candidatus Cibionibacter quicibialis", 
                       "C. C. quicibialis", name)) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Candidatus Avimonas narfia", 
                       "C. Avimonas narfia", name)) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Mediterraneibacter butyricigenes", 
                       "M. butyricigenes", name)) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Mediterraneibacter massiliensis", 
                       "M. massiliensis", name)) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Anaerotruncus rubiinfantis", 
                       "A. rubiinfantis", name)) %>% 
  mutate(name = ifelse(name == "Sutterella wadsworthensis", 
                       "S. wadsworthensis", name)) %>%
```
